questions.q: Which Constitutional Amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment of 1992 gave constitutional status to rural local self-government.

questions.q: In which year was the 73rd Amendment passed?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment was passed in 1992 and came into force in 1993.

questions.q: Panchayati Raj is a system of self-government primarily for which areas?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj provides for self-government in rural areas of India.

questions.q: How many tiers does the Panchayati Raj system have under the 73rd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment established a three-tier panchayat system at village, intermediate and district levels.

questions.q: The lowest tier of the Panchayati Raj system operates at which level?
questions.explanation: The Gram Panchayat operates at the village level, the lowest tier of the system.

questions.q: The 73rd Amendment mandated reservation of seats in panchayats for which group, among others?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandated reservation of seats for women and for weaker sections in panchayats.

questions.q: What is the village-level body of all registered voters in a panchayat area called?
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha is the assembly of all registered voters in a village within the panchayat area.

questions.q: The 73rd Amendment requires panchayat elections to be held:
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment mandates regular panchayat elections every five years.

questions.q: Panchayati Raj is associated with the idea of democracy at which level?
questions.explanation: Panchayati Raj is often described as democracy at the grassroots level.

questions.q: The head of a Gram Panchayat is commonly known as the:
questions.explanation: The elected head of a Gram Panchayat is commonly called the Sarpanch or Pradhan.

questions.q: The provisions relating to panchayats were added to the Constitution as which new Part?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX, titled 'The Panchayats', to the Constitution.

questions.q: Which Schedule was added to the Constitution by the 73rd Amendment listing subjects for panchayats?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule, listing 29 subjects for panchayats, was added by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.q: How many subjects are listed in the Eleventh Schedule for the panchayats?
questions.explanation: The Eleventh Schedule contains 29 subjects that may be devolved to panchayats.

questions.q: The intermediate tier of the Panchayati Raj system operates at which level?
questions.explanation: The intermediate tier, often called the Panchayat Samiti or Mandal Parishad, operates at the block level.

questions.q: The top tier of the Panchayati Raj system operates at which level?
questions.explanation: The top tier, the Zila Parishad, operates at the district level.

questions.q: The 73rd Amendment provided that the intermediate tier may be omitted in states with a population below approximately:
questions.explanation: States with a population not exceeding twenty lakh may choose not to constitute the intermediate-level panchayat.

questions.q: Which body recommended a three-tier Panchayati Raj system in 1957, influencing later reforms?
questions.explanation: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee of 1957 recommended a three-tier Panchayati Raj structure.

questions.q: Which state is widely recognised as the first to inaugurate the Panchayati Raj system, in 1959?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan was the first state to inaugurate Panchayati Raj, at Nagaur in 1959.

questions.q: Under the 73rd Amendment, who conducts elections to the panchayats?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment provides for a State Election Commission to conduct panchayat elections.

questions.q: The body that recommends the distribution of financial resources to panchayats within a state is the:
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment provides for a State Finance Commission to review the finances of panchayats every five years.

questions.q: What minimum proportion of seats in panchayats is reserved for women under the 73rd Amendment?
questions.explanation: Not less than one-third of seats in panchayats are reserved for women under the 73rd Amendment.

questions.q: The Panchayati Raj idea draws on the constitutional Directive Principle in Article 40, which directs the State to organise:
questions.explanation: Article 40, a Directive Principle, directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.q: Which committee, reporting in 1986, recommended giving constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The L.M. Singhvi Committee in 1986 recommended constitutional recognition for Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.q: Under Article 243-E, what is the maximum term of a panchayat?
questions.explanation: Article 243-E fixes the term of every panchayat at five years from the date of its first meeting.

questions.q: Which Act of 1996 extended the provisions of Part IX to the Scheduled Areas?
questions.explanation: The Panchayats (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, or PESA, of 1996 extended Part IX provisions to Scheduled Areas with modifications.

questions.q: Under Article 243-C, what is the minimum age to be eligible to be a member of a panchayat?
questions.explanation: A person must have attained the age of 21 years to be a member of a panchayat under Article 243-C.

questions.q: If a panchayat is dissolved before its term, an election to constitute a new one must be completed within what period?
questions.explanation: Article 243-E requires that elections to a dissolved panchayat be completed within six months of dissolution.

questions.q: Which range of Articles in the Constitution deals with the Panchayats under Part IX?
questions.explanation: Part IX of the Constitution covers panchayats through Articles 243 to 243-O.

questions.q: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in panchayats is to be in proportion to their:
questions.explanation: Article 243-D provides that seats reserved for SCs and STs are in proportion to their population in the panchayat area.

questions.q: The 73rd Amendment bars courts from interfering in panchayat electoral matters except through a petition filed:
questions.explanation: Article 243-O bars court interference in delimitation and election matters except by election petition as provided by state law.
